topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices.

doc1: impact of solar power on reducing carbon footprint
doc2: benefits of energy efficiency in urban areas
doc3: role of government policies in promoting sustainability
challenges in transitioning to green energy

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and reducing environmental impact.

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The topic focuses on innovative approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy production and consumption.

doc3: impact of wind energy on the environment

topic.name: Medicaid and Medicare Amendments
topic.description: Legislative amendments related to the enhancement and accessibility of healthcare services under Medicaid and Medicare programs.

Document 1: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
extends this 100% of Medicare payment floor
Document 2: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare)
authorize a Medicare part D eligible individual enrolled in the plan to request an exception
Document 3: Amends part A (General Provisions) of title XI of the Social Security Act
make Guam and the Virgin Islands eligible for the Supplemental Security Income program
Document 4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for the coverage of and payment for certified midwife services
Currently only certified nurse-midwife services are covered
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide coverage of diabetes prevention program services
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish the criteria for a diabetes prevention program
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a breast cancer treatment quality performance system
base payment to such providers for such treatment on their performance

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code addressing various tax-related issues including expensing, education savings, employment status, and agricultural loans.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal limitations on the expensing of depreciable business assets.
allow taxpayers to elect a two-year recovery period for depreciable property.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt the USAccount Fund and USAccounts from income taxation.
increase the amount of the child tax credit by the applicable USAccount contribution amount.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income interest received by a lender from real estate loans secured by agricultural real estate.
Agricultural real estate includes real property that is substantially used for the production of one or more agricultural products.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the classification of workers for employment tax purposes.
require taxpayers who hire independent contractors on a regular and ongoing basis to provide such contractors with notice of their federal tax obligations.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce the federal excise tax on gasoline and special fuels.
corresponding increases in fuel taxes imposed by states.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for contributions to a qualified tuition program.
allow an exclusion, up to $600, from the gross income of an employee for employer contributions to a 529 tuition program.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt donations of motor vehicles to charities from the limitations and substantiation requirements.
the charity does not regularly use the property or services of a third party in carrying out its vehicle donation program.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension and Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on various products and the regulatory framework for low-value shipments.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on reaction product of 3,5-dimethyl-1,2-diazole
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on polytetramethylene ether glycol
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Sulfur Black 1
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: increase from $200 to $800 the aggregate retail value
imported duty-free into the United States
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on footwear for women
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on potassium persulfate
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on the mixtures or coprecipitates of yttrium phosphate
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Defense and Intelligence Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to the Department of Defense and intelligence community operations, including oversight, appropriations, and operational guidelines.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on EMP weapons programs.
Identifies each country pursuing an EMP weapons program.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish a board of review for mental health determinations.
Ensures covered individuals receive notification of their right to a separation review.
Document 3: Revises the definition of 'entitlement land' for tax payments to local governments.
Includes federal land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Defense.
Document 4: Prohibits the use of armed UAVs by federal agencies other than DOD.
Authorizes only uniformed members of the Armed Forces to operate armed UAVs.
Document 5: Authorizes appropriations for intelligence activities of various defense and intelligence agencies.
Specifies amounts authorized for the conduct of intelligence-related activities.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Defense to develop guidelines for manufacturing supplies in U.S. arsenals.
Requires annual reporting to Congress on Army arsenal capabilities.
Document 7: Authorizes appropriations for intelligence activities for FY2009.
Specifies personnel ceilings for intelligence activities.

topic.name: Land Conservation and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the establishment, management, and conservation of wilderness areas, national parks, and public lands, focusing on ecological preservation, cultural significance, and sustainable use.

Document 1: Establishes Rio Grande Del Norte National Conservation Area in New Mexico for the purposes of conserving, protecting, and enhancing cultural, archaeological, natural, ecological, geological, historical, wildlife, educational, recreational, and scenic resources.
Requires the Secretary to ensure the protection of religious and cultural sites and to provide occasional access to them by tribal members for traditional cultural and customary uses.
Document 2: Amends the Oregon Wilderness Act of 1984 to designate certain lands in Siskiyou National Forest as the 'Copper Salmon Wilderness.'
Prohibits anything in this Act from being construed as diminishing any right of any Indian tribe.
Document 3: Directs the Departments of the Interior and the Air Force to enter into a memorandum of agreement for the continued management by Interior of approximately 625,643 acres of Bureau of Land Management (BLM) land in Utah.

The bill establishes the Utah Test and Training Range Community Resource Group to give input to Interior and the Air Force on matters involving public access to, use of, and management of the BLM land.
Document 4: Authorizes the Department of the Interior to award grants to wildlife or veterinary institutions to create additional clinical and research positions for wildlife and zoological veterinarians.

Interior must also establish a pilot program to award grants to accredited schools and colleges of veterinary medicine to develop or improve new or existing curricula that specialize in wildlife or zoological veterinary medicine.
Document 5: Directs the Department of the Interior to conduct sales of eligible lands in Mohave County, Arizona, which are administered by the Bureau of Land Management and have been designated for disposal by Interior.
Each sale of eligible land shall be through a competitive bidding process, under which adjoining landowners are offered the first option.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to consider anticipated tribal benefits and applicable state and local government concerns before taking off-reservation land into trust for Indian tribes.
Requires a tribe's proposed use of the land to be compatible with state and local planning and zoning, and public health and safety requirements.
Document 7: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Prohibits any use of motorized vehicles and other motorized machinery in the Area that was not permitted on March 1, 2006.

topic.name: Veterans' Health and Substance Abuse
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving health care services and addressing substance abuse issues among veterans.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to convene a Pain Management Best Practices Inter-Agency Task Force to develop best practices for pain management and prescribing pain medication.
Authorizes the Attorney General to make grants to states, local governments, and nonprofit organizations to expand educational efforts to prevent abuse of opioids.
Document 2: This bill amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to authorize the Department of Justice (DOJ) to award grants to state, local, and tribal governments to provide opioid abuse services.
DOJ's Office of Inspector General must conduct annual audits of selected grant recipients.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish a program to screen veterans eligible for Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) hospital, medical, and nursing home care for symptoms of traumatic brain injury (TBI).
Requires a program report from the Secretary to the congressional veterans' committees.
Document 4: Provides that if a veteran eligible for a pension disposes of a resource for less than its fair market value, then the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall deny or discontinue the pension payment.
Requires annual reports from the Secretary, through 2023, on the administration of this Act.
Document 5: Makes any veteran who was exposed in the line of duty to an occupational and environmental health chemical hazard eligible for hospital care, medical services, and nursing home care.
Notwithstanding insufficient medical evidence to conclude that the disability may be associated with such exposure.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Defense to provide a person-to-person mental health assessment for each member deployed in support of a contingency operation.
Allows the Secretary, in order to prevent suicide, self-harm, harm to others, or under-performance, to retire a member if the member is otherwise qualified for retirement.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to enter into an agreement with the Secretary of Defense for the transfer of data to provide members of the Armed Forces and veterans with individualized information concerning veterans' benefits.
Requires the Secretary to notify the member or veteran of such benefits.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to immigration, including detention, residency requirements, and the treatment of specific groups within the immigration system.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to revise requirements for the detention and removal of aliens ordered removed.
Expresses the sense of Congress that constitutional rights should be upheld and protected.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) bar to U.S. admission.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to review immigration policies regarding HIV as a communicable disease of public health significance.
Document 4: Confers 'blue card status' upon an alien who has performed specified lengths of agricultural employment in the United States.
Provides for adjustment of blue card status to permanent resident status based upon completed periods of agricultural employment.
Document 5: Provides permanent resident status adjustment for an applicant alien who was on September 10, 2001, the spouse, child, or unmarried son or daughter of an alien who died as a direct result of the September 11, 2001, terrorist activity.
Makes the provisions of this Act inapplicable to an alien who is inadmissible or deportable under criminal or security grounds.

topic.name: Food and Drug Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at regulating food and drug safety, including inspection protocols, approval processes, and safety standards.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to set forth provisions governing food safety
establish science-based minimum standards for the safe production and harvesting of fruits and vegetables
Document 2: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to revise provisions regarding review and approval of generic drug applications
The FDA must prioritize the review of such submissions and act on them within 180 days
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a program for consultation with external experts
inform and strengthen the Food and Drug Administration's (FDA's) review of drugs and biologic products for rare diseases
Document 5: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act, the Poultry Products Inspection Act and the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
provide the Secretary of Agriculture with authority for voluntary and mandatory nondistribution and recall
Document 6: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Food and Drug Administration to refer new drug applications for opioids
require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to provide support for activities related to opioid abuse

topic.name: Transportation Safety Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various regulations and acts aimed at improving safety and transparency in the transportation sector, particularly for air and rail carriers.

Document 1: Makes it an unfair or deceptive practice for... to fail to provide certain information to an online ticket purchaser...
Requires the Secretary of Transportation to issue regulations...
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Transportation to revise certain airline service quality performance reporting regulations...
Document 3: Suspends the authority of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to realign FAA air traffic control facilities...
Document 4: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to promulgate regulations prohibiting commercial flight crew members from using portable electronic devices...
Document 5: This bill extends deadlines and modifies requirements for railroad carriers... to implement positive train control (PTC) systems.
Document 6: Repeals the Essential Air Service Local Participation Program...
Document 7: Requires each rail carrier that is a Class I railroad... to develop and submit for Secretary of Transportation approval... a plan to implement a positive train control system...

topic.name: Unemployment Compensation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts related to unemployment compensation, employee rights, and labor regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act
Requires an employer to give 90-day written notice to employees
Document 2: Provides for federal-state agreements under which a state will make targeted extended unemployment compensation payments
Specifies criteria for Tier-1, Tier-2, and Tier-3 periods of increasing unemployment
Document 5: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation program
Document 7: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account
Postpones the termination of the program until June 8, 2013

topic.name: Child Welfare and Education Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving child welfare, education funding, and safety in schools and foster care systems.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to allocate funds to states for competitive matching grants to local educational agencies for school repair, renovation, and construction.
Requires each state, in awarding such grants to LEAs, to consider the percentage of poor children each LEA serves.
Document 2: Establishes within the Department of State the Office of Vulnerable Children and Family Security which shall support the implementation in foreign countries of child welfare laws and policies.
Represent the United States in matters relevant to international child welfare, family preservation and reunification.
Document 3: Amends the Indian Child Protection and Family Violence Prevention Act to prohibit the final approval of any foster care placement until the tribal social services agency completes a criminal records check.
Defines a 'covered individual' as an adult and any other individual the tribe determines is subject to a criminal records check.

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at supporting and regulating small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: This bill amends the Small Business Act to reauthorize for FY2017-FY2022 both the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Program and the Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) Program.
The SBA must report annually to Congress by June 30 on the use of SBIR program funds by federal agencies for specified purposes in the Act.
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to carry out a Small Loan Advantage Program.
Authorizes the Administrator to increase the Program's maximum loan amount, reduce the Program's fees, and further streamline the Program's loan approval process.
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to obtaining the best available collateral for a disaster loan.
Requires a report from the Administrator to specified congressional committees.
Document 4: This bill amends the Buy American Act to define the term 'substantially all'.

The bill requires each federal agency that applies an exception or grants a waiver to Buy American requirements to submit to the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) a notification of such exception or waiver.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, funding, and employee rights of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: replace at least 10% of the gasoline-powered motor vehicles in the Postal fleet with electric motor vehicles
ensure that at least 75% of the Postal fleet is comprised of such electric motor vehicles
Document 2: directs the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to make a determination or redetermination of postal surplus
requires OPM to redetermine the postal surplus or supplemental liability
Document 3: results of an AMP study shall not be cognizable for any purpose unless specified time limit and notice requirements are met
requires the U.S. Postal Service to ensure that no reduction in the workforce occurs while an AMP study is pending
Document 4: extends, for four years, the authority of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to issue a semipostal
contribute to funding for breast cancer research
Document 5: provides postal benefits to a member of the Armed Forces who is on active duty
postal-free mailings for service members
Document 6: extends federal employee protections against adverse personnel actions
right to appeal such actions to the Merit Systems Protection Board
Document 7: changes the days on which the POW/MIA flag is required to be displayed
to all days on which the U.S. flag is displayed

topic.name: Voting and Election Reforms
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving voter registration, protection, and election processes in the United States.

Document 1: permit same-day voter re-registration at polling places
require counting of provisional ballots
Document 2: require technological security measures to prevent addresses from appearing on voter registration lists
authorize Members of Congress to file an affidavit for home address
Document 3: require disclosure of poll funding sources
report poll costs to the Federal Election Commission
Document 5: prohibit conversion of leadership PAC funds to personal use
Document 6: establish procedures for collecting absentee ballots of overseas voters
ensure ballots are delivered prior to closing of polls
Document 7: establish a Constitution Day Grant Program
enhance educational programs that teach students about the U.S. Constitution and voting

topic.name: Health and Family Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on health, family planning, and support for workers and families.

Document 1: Requires certain covered employers to provide annually to each of their covered: (1) full-time employees at least seven days of paid sick time, and specified employment benefits;
Allows the covered employee to use such sick time for an absence: (1) resulting from a medical condition involving symptoms of a contagious illness.
Document 2: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program or family planning activity.
Document 3: Prohibit a physician from knowingly performing a dismemberment abortion.
A woman or a parent of a minor who undergoes a dismemberment abortion may file a civil action for damages against an individual who violates this bill.
Document 4: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to include lactation as protected conduct under such Act.
Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to extend the requirement that certain employers provide reasonable break time for an employee to express breast milk.
Document 5: Requires a person who performs an abortion to have admitting privileges at a local hospital and notify the patient of the location of the hospital.
Prohibits a woman upon whom an abortion is performed from being prosecuted for conspiracy to violate this Act.
Document 6: Requires the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to provide each individual whose personally identifiable information was compromised during data breaches with comprehensive identity protection coverage.
Includes not less than $5 million in identity theft insurance.
Document 7: Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 to authorize appropriations to carry out the Child Care and Development Block Grant program.
Requires the state plan to certify that the state has in effect licensing requirements applicable to all licensed and regulated child care providers.

topic.name: Federal Legislation and Governance
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of federal legislation, including judicial appointments, land management, advisory committees, marriage laws, industry regulations, trade secrets, and educational assistance for military personnel.

Document 1: requires the President to appoint, with the advice and consent of the Senate, two additional district judges for the district of Colorado.
Document 2: Withdraws the approximately 1,068,908 acres of the Tusayan Ranger District and federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM)... from all forms of entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws.
Document 3: amends the Federal Advisory Committee Act (FACA) to modify and expand requirements for federal advisory committees.
Document 4: denies federal courts jurisdiction to hear or decide any question pertaining to the interpretation of... the Defense of Marriage Act.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to issue orders applicable to manufacturers of concrete masonry products...
Document 6: authorizes an owner of a trade secret related to a product or service used in interstate or foreign commerce... to bring a civil action to obtain appropriate relief.
Document 7: Authorizes the commanding general of the District of Columbia National Guard to provide educational assistance to members of the District of Columbia National Guard...

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to energy production, environmental regulations, and financial oversight in the energy sector.

Document 1: Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to open certain areas in the Eastern Gulf of Mexico to oil and gas leasing.
Allocates funds among Gulf producing states and coastal political subdivisions.
Document 4: Makes certain emission limits for hydrogen chloride and sulfur dioxide inapplicable to an electric utility steam generating unit.
utilizes circulating fluidized bed technology to convert coal refuse into energy.
Document 6: Amends the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 to eliminate sequential and cumulative adjustments for inflation.
Prohibits adjustments for inflation or any other factor.

topic.name: Health Insurance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating health insurance policies, access, and related financial protections.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act, the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA), and the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from the definition of 'health insurance coverage' a stop loss policy obtained by a self-insured health plan.
to reimburse the plan or sponsor for losses incurred in providing health or medical benefits to such plan participants in excess of a predetermined level set forth in the stop loss policy.
Document 2: Prohibits discrimination based on a person's status as a living organ donor in the offering, issuance, cancellation, coverage, price, or any other condition of a life insurance policy.
Revises the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to specifically include living organ donation as a serious health condition that entitles a covered employee to leave under that Act.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for the amount paid by the taxpayer for qualified health insurance coverage, excluding any employer subsidized coverage.
Provides for the establishment and governance of individual membership associations (IMAs) to make available health benefits coverage to all members of the IMA.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at amending existing laws and establishing new regulations across diverse areas such as agriculture, criminal justice, consumer protection, and political transparency.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to exclude industrial hemp from the definition of 'marihuana.'
Defines 'industrial hemp' to mean the plant Cannabis sativa L.
Document 2: Amends the federal judicial code to establish in the U. S. Marshals Service a Short Term State Witness Protection Section.
Directs the Section to give priority in awarding grants and providing services to prosecutor's offices.
Document 3: Amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to direct the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF) to make a video recording of the entire process of its examination and testing.
Bars ATF from editing or erasing any such recording.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to make changes to the definition of 'color additive.'
Authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish alternative labeling requirements.
Document 5: Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings.
Directs a district court to order the face and voice of the witness to be disguised or otherwise obscured.
Document 6: Amends the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995 to require the disclosure of political intelligence activities.
Requires political intelligence consultants or their employing organizations to register with the Secretary of the Senate.
Document 7: Amends the USEC Privatization Act to permit an employee of the U.S. Enrichment Corporation's operating contractor to bring suit in federal district court.
for a violation of an agreement between an employer and a labor organization.

topic.name: Federal Support and Regulation Acts
topic.description: Legislation aimed at federal funding, support, and regulation in various sectors including education, energy, and fraud prevention.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to direct the Department of Education (ED) to support local educational agencies (LEAs), community colleges, and other appropriate entities in modernizing, renovating, or repairing facilities used to provide science, technology, engineering, mathematics, or career and technical education.
Requires ED to furnish such support by providing eligible entities with grants or loan guarantees.
Document 2: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to disseminate to seniors and their caregivers information on mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud targeting seniors.
Directs the Attorney General to establish a grant program for mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud prevention education programs for senior citizens.
Document 3: Amends the Treasury, Postal Service and General Government Appropriations Act, 1995, to require fees for services provided by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to be deposited in the general fund of the Treasury.
Currently, the Secretary of the Treasury may spend such fees to supplement IRS appropriations.
Document 4: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in design, planning, and construction of permanent facilities to reclaim and reuse water.
Limits the federal share of project costs to 25% of the total.
Document 5: Prohibits any funds appropriated or otherwise available for the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from being used to implement or enforce a cap-and-trade program.
Defines 'cap-and-trade program' as any regulatory program established after the date of enactment of this Act.
Document 6: Amends the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 to modify provisions authorizing the Cherokee Nation of Oklahoma to design and construct hydroelectric generating facilities.
Requires such Nation to obtain any permit required by federal or state law before the date on which construction begins on such facilities.
Document 7: Prohibits funds from any U.S. assessed or voluntary contribution to the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) from being used to support assistance provided by the IAEA through its Technical Cooperation Program to any country, including North Korea, that is a state sponsor of terrorism.
Directs the Secretary of State to withhold specified voluntary and assessed IAEA contribution amounts.

topic.name: Sustainable Development and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at promoting sustainable practices in housing, transportation, land conservation, and environmental protection.

Document 1: Establishes in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) an Office of Sustainable Housing and Communities (OSHC).
promote integrated transportation, housing, energy, and economic development activities carried out across policy and governmental jurisdictions.
Document 2: develop a national information clearinghouse on oil discharge.
provide for research, development, and demonstration of new or improved technologies which are effective in preventing, detecting, containing, recovering, or mitigating oil discharges.
Document 4: establish at least one Forest Active Management Area within each unit of the National Forest System designated for sustainable forest management.
provide a dependable source of payments to states in which a national forest is situated of 25% of specified forest receipts.

topic.name: Environmental and Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to environmental protection, energy production, and the management of natural resources.

Document 1: consolidate government Internet portals on employment for current and former members of the Armed Forces
connecting members who are seeking employment with employers
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to sell certain lands in Superior National Forest
consideration for a sale of lands to be not less than the appraised market value
Document 3: authorize the U.S. Geological Survey to conduct critical monitoring, scientific assessments, and research
support of fisheries within the Great Lakes Basin
Document 4: revise and reauthorize the program of research, development, demonstration, and commercial application to expand marine and hydrokinetic renewable energy production
support in-water testing and demonstration of marine and hydrokinetic renewable energy technologies
Document 5: prohibits the implementation of any program that grants temporary legal status
until the Secretary of Homeland Security certifies to the President and to Congress
Document 6: reauthorize part D (Enhancing Education through Technology)
require states to use a portion of their allotted funds to develop challenging academic content
Document 7: prohibit the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission from considering public use and environmental purposes
defines 'public use and environmental purposes' to mean the purposes of energy conservation
